data.title: Avro India Details Conduct of Virtual Extraordinary General Meeting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

AVRO India Ltd. ( (IN:AVROIND) ) has issued an update.

Avro India Ltd. informed exchanges that it convened an Extraordinary General Meeting on April 18, 2026, conducted entirely via video conferencing and other audio-visual means in line with Companies Act and SEBI regulations. The company detailed compliance with MCA and SEBI circulars, including electronic dispatch of the EGM notice to eligible shareholders and restricting attendance to direct participants without proxies.

The meeting, which ran from 1:00 p.m. to 1:28 p.m. IST, recorded the presence of 32 members along with the board’s key directors, the chief financial officer, the company secretary, and the secretarial auditor. With quorum confirmed and the chairman presiding, the orderly conduct of the EGM underscores the company’s adherence to mandated governance processes and remote participation norms for listed entities.

More about AVRO India Ltd.

The latest announcement is out from Tata Capital Ltd. ( (IN:TATACAP) ).

Tata Capital Limited has scheduled a media conference call on 23 April 2026 at 5:30 p.m. IST to discuss its audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended 31 March 2026. The company will subsequently hold an earnings call with analysts and investors the same day from 6:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. IST, with audio recordings and transcripts to be made available on its website, underscoring its emphasis on transparency and regular engagement with capital market participants.

The calls, to be led by Managing Director and CEO Rajiv Sabharwal along with Chief Financial Officer Rakesh Bhatia and the senior management team, are intended to brief stakeholders on quarterly and annual performance. By formally notifying the stock exchanges and providing structured access for media and investors, Tata Capital is reinforcing its compliance with disclosure regulations and its efforts to maintain clear communication with shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Tata Capital Ltd.

Tata Capital Limited is a diversified financial services company in India, offering lending, investment, and other financial solutions to retail, corporate, and institutional clients. As part of the Tata Group, it focuses on serving the Indian capital and credit markets through a range of financing and advisory products.

The latest update is out from Lupin Limited ( (IN:LUPIN) ).

Lupin Limited has reported that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has completed an inspection of its manufacturing facility in Somerset, New Jersey, conducted from April 13 to April 17, 2026, and closed the review with a Form 483 containing three observations. Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd ( (IN:KANORICHEM) ) has issued an announcement.

Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd has approved the allotment of 7% non-convertible, non-cumulative, non-participating, redeemable preference shares with a face value of Rs. 1,000 each. The issue, structured as a preferential allotment on a private placement basis, will see 495,000 such shares issued to promoter group entity R. V. Investment and Dealers Limited for an aggregate consideration of Rs. 49.5 crore.

The move represents a targeted capital-raising step that strengthens the company’s funding base without diluting equity shareholding. By channeling funds from a promoter group entity via redeemable preference shares, Kanoria Chemicals reinforces promoter support while optimizing its capital structure, a development that may be viewed positively by creditors and other stakeholders.

More about Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd

Kanoria Chemicals & Industries Ltd is an Indian chemical manufacturer based in Kolkata, engaged in producing industrial chemicals and related products for diverse end-use sectors. The company is listed on both the National Stock Exchange of India and BSE, giving it access to public capital markets and a broad base of institutional and retail investors.

data.title: Coforge Sees Senior M&A and Investor Relations Leaders Step Down After Major Expansion Drive
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Coforge Limited ( (IN:COFORGE) ) has provided an update.

Coforge Limited has announced the resignation of Anup Kumar, Executive Vice President and Global Head of M&A, and Manish Hemrajani, Head of Investor Relations, both effective at the close of business on April 17, 2026 in the U.S. The management changes come after a period in which Coforge executed five acquisitions, integrated Cigniti, divested its non-core AdvantageGo business, and scaled revenues from $1.1 billion in May 2025 to $2.5 billion post-Encora, underscoring a significant M&A-driven expansion phase whose leadership will now transition.

In his resignation note, Kumar highlighted that his decision stems from a desire to pursue an entrepreneurial path, while expressing confidence in Coforge’s continued growth as the Encora deal nears closing following regulatory approvals. The twin departures mark a shift in senior leadership at a time when the company is digesting major transactions and elevated scale, making succession in M&A and investor relations critical for sustaining its growth trajectory and maintaining market confidence.

More about Coforge Limited

Coforge Limited is an India-headquartered IT services and digital transformation company serving global enterprises across sectors. The firm focuses on technology-led solutions, including application development, integration, and specialized domain platforms, and has expanded its scale through a combination of organic growth and strategic acquisitions in key international markets.

Linedata Services ( (FR:LIN) ) has shared an update.

Linedata Services has announced that its 2025 Universal Registration Document, which includes the Annual Financial Report for the year ended 31 December 2025, has been filed with the French markets regulator and is now available on both the company’s website and the regulator’s portal, as well as at its registered office. data.title: Polestar Earnings Call: Growth Surges Amid Heavy Losses
data.description:
Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC Sponsored ADR Class C ((PSNYW)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Polestar Automotive’s latest earnings call painted a mixed picture for investors. Management highlighted record volumes, strong revenue growth and notable progress on adjusted margins, but these achievements were overshadowed by heavy impairment charges, persistent GAAP losses and sizable cash burn, leaving the turnaround story promising yet clearly risky.

Record Retail Sales and Volume Growth

Polestar delivered more than 60,100 cars in FY2025, up 34% year on year and squarely within its 30%–35% guidance range. Q4 retail sales climbed 27% to about 15,600 units, while Q1 2026 reached roughly 13,100 cars, marking the company’s strongest first quarter ever.

Strong Revenue Growth

Revenue exceeded USD 3.0 billion in FY2025, a surge of 50% from the prior year driven by higher volumes, a richer model mix and rising carbon credit income. Q4 revenue jumped 54% year on year to USD 887 million, underscoring solid commercial traction despite tough market conditions.

Improvement in Adjusted Profitability Metrics

Adjusted gross margin for 2025 improved sharply to -0.7% from -12.5% in 2024, with Q4 margins turning positive at 2% versus a deeply negative level a year earlier. Adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed by about USD 300 million to -USD 783 million for the year, and Q4 adjusted EBITDA improved to -USD 223 million from -USD 470 million.

Capital Raises and Strong Shareholder Support

The company secured about USD 1.2 billion of new equity between June 2025 and March 2026, including roughly USD 1.0 billion since December. Equity issued at USD 19.34 per share boosted free float above 40%, while plans to convert around USD 640 million of shareholder loans into equity aim to bolster liquidity and reduce leverage.

Network Expansion and Distribution Momentum

Polestar expanded its retail footprint by about 50% in 2025, growing from 140 to 210 sales points and targeting roughly 250 by year end, implying another 20% increase. The Polestar 4 has emerged as the volume engine, accounting for about 65%–66% of recent sales and anchoring the brand in key markets.

Product and Technology Advancements

Management touted its largest product offensive yet, with four new models planned over the next three years, including the Polestar 5, a new Polestar 4 variant, a next‑generation Polestar 2 and the compact Polestar 7. Upgrades to the Polestar 3, such as an 800V architecture enabling up to 350–500 kW charging and a major NVIDIA compute jump, seek to keep the brand technologically competitive.

Cost Discipline and Product Cost Reductions

Headcount fell by about 25% in 2025, contributing to SG&A savings of USD 34 million and total cost reductions of roughly USD 100 million, or 12% year on year. Product costs also moved lower, with the Polestar 4 benefiting from low double‑digit percentage cost cuts via negotiations and selective decontenting while maintaining a premium positioning.

Liquidity and Facility Access

Polestar ended December 2025 with around USD 1.2 billion in cash, offering a liquidity cushion amid ongoing losses. The company also secured roughly USD 1.6 billion of new 12‑month term facilities and renewed about USD 3.0 billion of existing lines, staying in covenant compliance and agreeing amendments for 2026.

Large Impairment Charges and Negative GAAP Margins

Despite operational progress, the P&L was hit by about USD 1.1 billion of impairment charges tied to Polestar 2, Polestar 3 and internal projects including Polestar 5. These write‑downs drove GAAP gross margin to a deeply negative 35% for the year and roughly -38% in Q4, masking underlying margin improvements.

Continued Net Losses and Adjusted EBITDA Deficit

Net losses remained heavy, with Q4 alone posting a USD 799 million deficit, largely due to impairments but still reflecting structural unprofitability. Even on an adjusted basis, EBITDA losses of USD 783 million for 2025 and USD 223 million in Q4 point to continued negative operating cash flow and a long path to break-even.

Pressure on Pricing and Market Dynamics

Management acknowledged that pricing pressure and intense competition weighed on margins, even as product mix improved. Higher duties and tariffs lifted production costs and eroded profitability, underscoring the vulnerability of premium EV makers to policy and trade shifts.

U.S. Market Weakness and Regulatory Headwinds

The U.S. business deteriorated, with its share of retail volumes falling to about 7% in 2025 from 14% in 2024. Higher tariffs, changing policies and the loss or reduction of key incentives and credits cut into sales and hurt the North American revenue mix, complicating Polestar’s global growth story.

Rising Selling Costs and Investment Spend

Sales agent compensation increased by USD 65 million in 2025, broadly in line with rising volumes but still contributing to higher selling costs. R&D spending doubled to USD 78 million as Polestar invested more in new programs and capitalized less, lifting near‑term cash outflows but supporting future products.

High Interest Expense and Legacy CapEx Outflows

The company highlighted a substantial interest burden and residual capital expenditure payments from earlier programs that continued to weigh on cash flow in 2025. While planned debt‑to‑equity conversions should ease financing costs over time, these legacy outflows remain a near‑term drag on free cash generation.

Material Impairment Volatility

Q4 impairment charges dropped to USD 340 million from USD 622 million a year earlier, but their recurring scale signaled ongoing asset valuation risks. Management linked this volatility to fluctuating demand, tariffs and regulatory changes, suggesting earnings will remain sensitive to external shocks.

Cash Burn and Working Capital Pressures

Average cash burn ran at about USD 120 million, underlining the company’s reliance on external funding despite efficiency gains. Inventory reductions of roughly 7,000 vehicles helped working capital, but higher receivables and catch‑up payments on payables offset some of the benefit.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Outlook

For 2026, Polestar is guiding to low double‑digit retail sales growth, supported by an expanded network of about 250 sales points and a continued shift toward the Polestar 4 and new models such as the Polestar 5. Management expects similar three‑digit‑million carbon credit revenue, an improved cash burn profile and a stronger balance sheet following equity raises and loan conversions, though risks from competition and regulation persist.

Polestar’s earnings call showed a company pushing hard on growth, technology and cost control while battling sizable losses and volatile impairments. Investors must weigh improving operational trends and strengthened liquidity against ongoing cash burn, U.S. weakness and policy headwinds as the brand navigates a crowded global EV market.

data.title: F.N.B. Corp Earnings Call Highlights Profitable Growth
data.description:
F.N.B. Corp ((FNB)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

F.N.B. Corp’s latest earnings call struck a distinctly upbeat tone, with management highlighting broad-based strength in earnings, balance sheet health and capital returns even as they acknowledged near-term margin and cost headwinds. Leadership framed elevated investment in technology, data and customer-facing initiatives as a deliberate trade-off to support durable growth, efficiency gains and shareholder value over the longer term.

Strong Profitability and EPS Growth

F.N.B. reported net income of $137 million and diluted EPS of $0.38, marking a robust 19% increase from a year earlier. Management emphasized that the bottom-line advance reflects continued profitability and positive operating leverage, underscoring that revenue is growing faster than expenses despite a competitive banking backdrop.

PPNR and Operating Leverage

Pre-provision net revenue rose 17% year-over-year, signaling healthy core earnings power before credit costs. This performance translated into nearly 5% positive operating leverage, suggesting F.N.B. is successfully scaling its franchise and extracting efficiency gains while still funding strategic growth investments.

Loan Growth Acceleration

Total loans and leases ended the quarter at $35.1 billion, representing 3.9% annualized growth versus the prior quarter. Both consumer and commercial lending contributed, with about $198 million of consumer originations and $136 million of commercial loans, and C&I balances showing more than 4% annualized spot growth.

Deposit Stability and Loan-to-Deposit Ratio

Spot total deposits reached $38.9 billion, increasing $142 million sequentially and signaling stable funding in a tight market. Noninterest-bearing deposits grew at a 3.6% annualized pace and remained a healthy 26% of the mix, helping keep the loan-to-deposit ratio steady at about 90% and supporting disciplined balance sheet management.

Capital Strength and Shareholder Returns

Capital levels remain solid, with a CET1 ratio of 11.4% and tangible common equity near 9%, providing meaningful flexibility for growth and capital deployment. Reflecting this strength, the board authorized an additional $250 million in share repurchases, leaving $300 million of capacity, and lifted the quarterly dividend by 8% to $0.13 per share.

Tangible Book Value and ROATCE

Tangible book value per share climbed 11% year-over-year to $12.06, underscoring steady value creation for investors even through a choppy rate cycle. Return on average tangible common equity registered a strong 13.2%, showing that F.N.B. is generating attractive returns on its capital base while still investing for future growth.

Net Interest Income and Margin Momentum

Net interest income increased nearly 11% compared with the prior year, confirming that higher earning assets and disciplined pricing are offsetting modest margin pressure. While the first-quarter net interest margin dipped 3 basis points sequentially to 3.25%, management pointed to a March exit margin around 3.30% and signaled expectations for gradual expansion over the remainder of the year.

Fee Revenue Growth and Business Diversification

Noninterest income rose 3.7% to $91 million, demonstrating the benefits of diversification beyond spread income. Capital markets revenue jumped 27.8% to $6.8 million and wealth management fees edged up 2.8% to $21.8 million, and leadership sees further upside from investment banking, public finance, treasury services and wealth initiatives.

Efficiency and Expense Discipline

The efficiency ratio improved to 56.1% from 58.5% a year ago, indicating that revenue growth is outpacing the rise in operating costs. Management stressed that this improvement came even as the company stepped up spending on technology and growth platforms, reinforcing its narrative of balancing near-term cost discipline with long-term strategic investment.

Asset Quality and Reserves

Credit quality remained resilient, with a funded provision of $19.4 million supporting loan growth and modest charge-offs. Reserves held steady at $443 million, or 1.26% of loans, and rise to about 1.32% when including unamortized discounts, while nonperforming loans are covered nearly four times by reserves, giving F.N.B. a substantial buffer against potential weakness.

Strategic Wins and Innovation Initiatives

Management highlighted several strategic wins, including becoming an exclusive retail and tower services provider for Penn State, which strengthens regional presence and brand visibility. The bank also rolled out innovative services like foreign-currency-capable ATMs at Pittsburgh International Airport and continues to invest in AI, digital platforms and a 360-degree customer-view product, underpinning its customer-first strategy.

NIM Pressure from Fed Timing and Seasonal Funding

The modest first-quarter NIM decline was largely attributed to the timing of late-2025 Fed moves and predictable seasonal deposit outflows that required more short-term wholesale funding. Executives framed these pressures as temporary and pointed to the stronger March exit margin as evidence that spreads are already starting to normalize as funding dynamics improve.

Deposit Pricing Pressure and Competition

Management acknowledged sustained deposit pricing pressure across the industry as banks compete aggressively for commercial and corporate relationships. F.N.B. reported a cumulative spot deposit beta of roughly 27% since the start of the rate-cut cycle and plans continued targeted efforts to reduce overall funding costs while still defending and growing key client deposits.

Rising Noninterest Expense from Investments

Noninterest expense increased 4.5% year-over-year to $257.9 million, reflecting both growth and elevated investment levels, particularly in technology, facilities and customer programs. Occupancy and equipment costs rose 11%, while other expenses climbed 30% on higher fraud losses, litigation and mortgage down payment support, and management now expects full-year costs to land toward the top of its guidance range.

CRE Payoffs and Concentration Trends

Commercial real estate balances shrank by $110 million sequentially due to anticipated payoffs, highlighting an intentional effort to manage risk in a sensitive portfolio. Even with this decline, CRE exposure remains elevated at around 194% of Tier 1 capital plus allowance, though management expects this metric to continue easing before settling at a more comfortable longer-term level.

Modest Increases in Delinquencies and NPLs

Credit metrics showed slight but manageable deterioration, with delinquencies, nonperforming loans and OREO each up about 3 basis points sequentially. Overall delinquency sits near 74 basis points and NPLs around 34 basis points, while net charge-offs remain low at 18 basis points, prompting management to stay vigilant but not alarmed about emerging credit trends.

Geopolitical Uncertainty and Qualitative Overlays

F.N.B. kept qualitative overlays in its reserve framework tied to ongoing conflict in the Middle East and broader geopolitical and trade-policy risks. While these issues have not yet produced material credit stress in the portfolio, management signaled that they remain key watch factors that could influence future provisioning decisions if conditions deteriorate.

Regulatory and Capital Risk from Proposed Rules

Executives flagged proposed Basel III and related regulatory capital changes as a meaningful long-term variable for the sector, noting that final rules could alter optimal capital structures and deployment. The company has not baked potential impacts into current capital-return plans, leaving room to adjust buybacks or growth strategies if the new framework ultimately proves more demanding.

Guidance and Outlook

Looking ahead, F.N.B. reaffirmed its 2026 outlook for mid-single-digit growth in period-end loans and deposits and guided full-year net interest income to between $1.495 billion and $1.535 billion, with second-quarter NII expected at $370–$380 million. Noninterest income is projected at $370–$390 million, expenses at $1.00–$1.02 billion with strong positive leverage, and provision expense at $85–$105 million, supported by a March exit margin of 3.30% and robust capital and buyback capacity.

F.N.B.’s call painted a picture of a bank leaning into growth and innovation while keeping credit quality and capital strength firmly intact. Earnings momentum, diversified revenue and stable funding underpin a constructive outlook, though investors will be watching deposit costs, expense discipline and regulatory developments as key swing factors in the quarters ahead.
